title: Pre-registered strategy hypothesis spec: Trend + Support/Resistance + EMA/RSI confirmation (NQ daily, long-only v1)
type: STRATEGY HYPOTHESIS / SPECIFICATION ONLY (pre-registration). NO engine, NO backtest, NO results, NO optimization.
status: SPEC-ONLY. No backtest has been run. No code has been written for this branch.

weaknesses_this_branch_must_avoid: Weak raw entry edge: Donchian breakout entries fire AFTER an extended move, so the immediate forward window carries mean-reversion drag (S24: NO_EDGE at all horizons 5/10/20/40, IS and OOS). FIX: enter on a PULLBACK/retest toward support inside an established trend, not on breakout extension.

Dependence on a few large winners: removing the top 3 trades flips both IS (-3.82R) and OOS (-1.93R) negative; OOS best trade was 50.3% of net (S25). FIX: use a fixed 2R take-profit so outcomes are distributed rather than fat-tail-dependent, and add an explicit no-top-3-dependence PASS gate.

Sequence fragility: realized Donchian drawdown sat at ~11th percentile of trade-order shuffles; IS bootstrap had ~21% probability of total R <= 0 (S25 FRAGILE). FIX: require sequence-risk to be NOT FRAGILE as a pre-fixed gate.

Low OOS trade count: Donchian produced only 16 OOS trades, too small for confidence (the live WATCH trigger). FIX: pre-fix minimum IS/OOS trade-count thresholds and treat sub-threshold OOS as an automatic WATCH.

new_hypothesis.statement: In an established daily uptrend, a controlled pullback toward recent support that shows momentum stabilizing (RSI in a mid band, then turning up or price reclaiming EMA20) offers a positive-expectancy, distributed-outcome long entry, with risk fixed at 2N and reward capped at 2R. The hypothesized edge is in BUYING THE RETEST (mean-reversion-into-trend), the opposite of Donchian's breakout-extension entry whose edge was not supported.
new_hypothesis.direction_rationale: Long-only minimizes degrees of freedom and avoids asymmetric short-side overfit on a single market; NQ daily has a strong secular up-drift, so a long-only trend-pullback test is the cleanest first read. A symmetric short version is deferred to a later branch with its own fresh OOS, only if v1 survives.

market_and_timeframe.market_v1: NQ continuous daily bars (NQ.c.0, UTC-calendar daily) - same data construction already validated in S23-D13/D16.
market_and_timeframe.later_robustness_note: Multi-market robustness is D9 only, and only if the candidate survives. NOT in D1.
market_and_timeframe.timeframe_v1: Daily bars first - the existing offline data pipeline is daily and already structural-QA validated.
market_and_timeframe.intraday_note: Intraday is a separate future branch, not now.

indicators.ATR20: Wilder ATR, period 20 (same N construction as the frozen Donchian engine)
indicators.support_ref: rolling 20-day low = lowest low over the prior 20 bars EXCLUDING the current bar (bars[i-20:i])

fixed_rules.indicator_warmup: No signal may fire until all indicators are fully warm: i >= 200 (EMA200) and ATR20/RSI14 seeded. Use prior-bar-only data; no lookahead.
fixed_rules.trend_filter_long: close[i] > EMA200[i] AND EMA50[i] > EMA200[i] (bullish stack).

support_method_choice.chosen: Option B - rolling 20-day low as the support reference zone
rejected: Option A prior-20-day swing high/low (needs fractal/pivot detection - more DOF)
Option C rolling pivot levels (needs lookback+lookforward window - extra parameters and lookahead risk)
support_method_choice.why: The rolling 20-day low is fully deterministic, parameter-light, and lookahead-safe (prior bars only). It is a transparent proxy for recent support and requires no subjective chart drawing. NOTE: this REUSES a Donchian-style level only as a support REFERENCE; the entry logic is the OPPOSITE of Donchian (we buy a pullback toward support, not a breakout above resistance), so it does not inherit Donchian's NOT_SUPPORTED entry edge.

all_conditions_must_hold_on_signal_bar_i: C1 Trend: close[i] > EMA200[i] AND EMA50[i] > EMA200[i]
C2 Support proximity (pullback occurred): low[i] <= support_ref[i] + 1.5 * ATR20[i]  (price has retraced to within 1.5 ATR of the prior 20-day low)
C4 Confirmation/turn: RSI14[i] > RSI14[i-1]  OR  close[i] > EMA20[i]  (momentum turning up OR price reclaiming EMA20)
entry_long.execution: Enter at the OPEN of the NEXT bar (i+1). Fully lookahead-safe: all conditions use data through bar i close; fill at bar i+1 open.
entry_long.no_subjective_inputs: No hand-drawn levels, no discretionary zones. All thresholds are fixed constants.

stop.chosen_rule: Hard stop = entry_price - 2.0 * ATR20[i] (ATR from signal bar i). 1R = 2.0 * ATR20 = the initial stop distance.
stop.rejected_alt: support-minus-ATR-buffer stop (rejected to keep ONE fixed deterministic rule and a clean constant 1R for R-only accounting).
stop.intrabar: Hard stop is checked using bar low (long); management begins the bar AFTER entry; stop checked BEFORE target (conservative).

exit.primary_exit: Fixed +2R take-profit target = entry_price + 2.0 * (2.0 * ATR20[i]) ... i.e. +2R where 1R = 2N stop distance.
exit.risk_exit: Hard stop at -1R (2N) as defined above.
exit.secondary_backstop: Trend-invalidation: if still open at a bar CLOSE and close < EMA50, exit at that close (avoids dead money / large trend-reversal giveback).
precedence_per_bar: 1) intrabar: hard stop (-1R) checked first (bar low)
3) on close, if still open: EMA50 trend-break close exit
exit.why_capped_target: A fixed 2R cap is the deliberate antidote to S25 single-winner dependence: it distributes outcomes instead of relying on rare fat-tailed runners. This intentionally tests a DIFFERENT edge hypothesis (repeatable distributed R from retests) than Donchian's let-winners-run profile.

risk_accounting.unit: R-only (1R = initial 2N stop distance). No dollars, point value, costs, or roll in v1.
risk_accounting.position_handling: One position at a time; new signals ignored while in a position (flat-state entries only).

data_windows.oos_treatment: SEALED / unseen until the D4 OOS protocol is committed.
data_windows.anti_overfit_window_rule: Same as S23: NO parameter changes after OOS is seen; run OOS ONCE; no subperiod cherry-picking.

validation_ladder.D2: Implement engine + tests (new module files; do NOT modify Donchian source). Stdlib-first, offline, deterministic, lookahead-safe.
validation_ladder.D4: Pre-register the sealed OOS protocol and COMMIT it BEFORE any OOS run (mirror S23-D15).
validation_ladder.D5: Run OOS 2023-2025 exactly ONCE, judged strictly against the D4 protocol.
validation_ladder.D6: Entry-rule significance on the new entries (reuse engine/signal_significance). Must clear the NOT_SUPPORTED bar.
validation_ladder.D7: Trade-order Monte Carlo + bootstrap sequence-risk (mirror S25). Must clear FRAGILE.
validation_ladder.D8: Regime breakdown (trend vs chop / by year / by volatility).
validation_ladder.D9: Multi-market robustness (MNQ/ES/MES) as FRESH future OOS - only if the candidate has survived D5-D8.

pass_watch_fail_criteria.note: Pre-fixed BEFORE any test is run. Precedence FAIL -> WATCH -> PASS, same as S23-D15.

trade_count.oos_below_threshold_action: Automatic WATCH (this is exactly the trigger that held Donchian at WATCH; not repeating that mistake silently).

PASS_all_must_hold: OOS max drawdown <= 1.5 x IS max DD
majority of IS years positive AND at least one positive OOS year
no top-3 winner dependence: OOS total R remains POSITIVE after removing the top 3 winners
D6 entry significance is NOT ENTRY_EDGE_NOT_SUPPORTED
D7 sequence risk is NOT SEQUENCE_RISK_FRAGILE
WATCH_any_trigger: OOS trades between 1 and 29 (below confidence threshold)
removing top 3 winners leaves OOS only marginally positive
one year strongly dominates the result
D6 entry significance INCONCLUSIVE (not SUPPORTED, not NOT_SUPPORTED)
FAIL_any_trigger: OOS max drawdown materially exceeds 1.5 x IS max DD without recovery
removing top 3 winners flips OOS NEGATIVE (the exact Donchian S25 failure)
all OOS years negative OR structural data issue

anti_overfit_guardrails: No parameter sweeps in v1 - every constant above is FIXED before testing.
No changing the support/resistance definition (Option B) after seeing any result.
No adding filters after OOS is seen.
No tuning RSI band, ATR multiple, EMA lengths, or target multiple to results.
If a change is genuinely needed, it becomes a NEW separately-labeled branch with a fresh pre-registered OOS - never a retrofit of this one.
IS and OOS kept separate; no pooling to manufacture significance.
Donchian source files, reports, and verdicts are untouched and not re-run.
proposed_next_step: S26-D2: implement the engine and unit tests in NEW module files (e.g. engine/trend_sr_ema_rsi.py + tests), without modifying any Donchian source. No backtest results in D2 beyond test fixtures.
clear_line: NO BACKTEST HAS BEEN RUN. This is a pre-registered hypothesis/specification only. No engine code for this branch exists yet. No optimization, no results, no trading or paper claims.
